title: Dryopteris consanguinea
intro: Dryopteris consanguinea, also known as the autumn fern, is a species of fern in the Dryopteridaceae family, characterized by its deciduous fronds and native habitats in East Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiviral properties, helping to alleviate symptoms of fever, sore throats, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Dryopteris consanguinea include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the autumn fern is often prepared as a tea or used in infusions to treat various health conditions, although its use should be guided by a healthcare professional due to potential side effects and interactions.
taxonomy: Dryopteris consanguinea is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Dryopteris and the kingdom Plantae. This species is further classified under the division Pteridophyta and the phylum Tracheophyta. The class Polypodiopsida and the order Polypodiales are also part of its taxonomic classification. It is a type of wood fern native to parts of Asia.
uses: Dryopteris consanguinea is a species of fern that has been used in traditional medicine. Its fronds and rhizome are said to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Traditionally, it has been used to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The plant's extracts have also been studied for their potential antioxidant and antimicrobial activities. More research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Dryopteris consanguinea for medicinal purposes.
benefits: Dryopteris consanguinea is a species of fern known for its potential health benefits. The plant is rich in antioxidants and has anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and improve overall health. preparations: Dryopteris consanguinea, also known as the Chinese shield fern, has been used in traditional herbal medicine for its potential health benefits.
